entries.value: Target content engagement rate is >2%. Below 1.5% indicates content-audience mismatch or poor distribution. Above 5% is exceptional and signals viral potential.
Target email open rate is >25%. Industry average is 21%. Below 15% suggests subject line issues or list fatigue. Segment and A/B test subject lines to improve.
Target click-through rate is >3%. Below 1.5% indicates weak CTAs or content-offer mismatch. Test CTA placement, copy, and offer relevance.
Lead generation cost varies by industry. B2B SaaS benchmark is $30-$150 per lead. Track cost per channel to identify most efficient acquisition paths.
Target social reach growth is >5% month-over-month. Flat growth signals content staleness. Declining reach may indicate algorithm changes or audience saturation.
Target campaign ROI is >300% (3:1 return). Below 200% is unsustainable for paid channels. Content marketing ROI improves over time as assets compound organic traffic.
Maintain a 4-6 week content calendar. Map content to buyer journey stages: awareness (blog posts, social), consideration (whitepapers, webinars), decision (case studies, demos).
Campaign budget utilization should be 85-95% at campaign end. Under 80% indicates poor planning. Over 100% requires approval workflows. Review weekly.
Every long-form content asset should be repurposed into 5-8 social posts, 1 email sequence, and 1 infographic. This maximizes ROI per content investment.
Leads scoring 70+ are sales-qualified (SQL). Leads 40-69 enter nurture sequences. Below 40 remain in awareness campaigns. Re-score monthly based on engagement.
